steps.title: Translate the 3% annual increase
steps.content: The initial population is $18{,}000$, so the coefficient should be $18{,}000$. A $3\%$ yearly increase means each year is multiplied by $1+0.03=1.03$.

steps.title: Build the exponential model
steps.content:
After $x$ years, the factor $1.03$ is applied $x$ times:
$$p=18{,}000(1.03)^x$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content: The equations $p=0.97{ \left(18,000\right)}^{x}$ and $p=1.03{ \left(18,000\right)}^{x}$ put $18{,}000$ in the exponential base and misuse the initial population. The equation $p=18,000{ \left(0.97\right)}^{x}$ uses $0.97$, which would be a decrease. The model $p=18,000{ \left(1.03\right)}^{x}$ is correct, so D is the answer.
